qa_pairs.question: What are MPI compiler-wrappers and what is their purpose?
qa_pairs.answer: MPI compiler-wrappers are not compilers themselves; rather, they are scripts provided by MPI library packages that call other compilers while automatically passing MPI-specific flags. Their purpose is to simplify the compilation process for parallel programs in a distributed memory environment.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user determine which specific compiler and associated options are being utilized by an Open MPI compiler-wrapper?
qa_pairs.answer: With Open MPI, a user can use the `--showme` option with the compiler wrapper (e.g., `mpicc --showme`). This command will display the underlying compiler that will be called, as well as all the MPI-specific compiler-options that are automatically added.
